topics: Labor policy and prevailing wage trade-offs
Local government resistance to state housing mandates
Prop 13 and fiscalization of land use

claims.claim: The cost per square foot of constructing an apartment in California is over twice that of constructing it in Texas, according to a RAND study.
evidence: RAND study cited by Ezra Klein
California multifamily takes 49 months vs 27 months in Texas and 37 months in Colorado

claims.claim: Speed of permitting and approval—not labor or materials alone—is the primary driver of higher construction costs in California.
evidence: RAND study shows California takes 49 months vs Colorado's 27 months
Faster timelines could cut 10-20% off market-rate housing price
Katie Porter argues others haven't read the RAND study closely enough

claims.claim: Paying prevailing wage in residential construction increases the cost per unit by approximately $94,000.
evidence: Analysis from the Turner Center cited by Ezra Klein

claims.claim: Affordable housing costs roughly four times as much per square foot to construct in California as market-rate housing costs in Texas.
evidence: RAND study data cited by Ezra Klein: market-rate California is 2x Texas; affordable housing in California is 4x Texas market-rate

claims.claim: San Jose approved 22,000 homes that largely were not built because the economics didn't work, but cutting fees by over two-thirds led to 2,000 homes getting under construction in one year.
evidence: Mayor Matt Mahan's account of San Jose fee reductions
2,000 additional homes securing financing after fee cuts

claims.claim: Local impact fees add 10-20% to the cost of housing and are a primary lever that cities and counties control.
evidence: Matt Mahan cites over 10 pages of fees in San Jose
Tom Steyer states cities charge fees up to 20% of construction cost
Antonio Villaraigosa says impact fees are 'killing us'

claims.claim: Modular/offsite construction companies have repeatedly failed because they lacked sufficient orders, not because the technology doesn't work.
evidence: Katerra raised $2 billion in private capital and went bankrupt
Factory OS was rebranded and recapitalized
Tom Steyer argues state orders and building code changes can fix the revenue problem

claims.claim: There are 40,000 permitted and zoned housing units in San Francisco that are not being built because construction costs make them financially unviable.
evidence: Cited by both Tom Steyer and Javier Becerra
Becerra links the $10 billion Wicks bond to unlocking these shovel-ready projects

quote_candidates.text: A RAND study found that the cost per square foot of constructing an apartment in California is over twice that of constructing it in Texas.
quote_candidates.speaker: Ezra Klein

quote_candidates.text: There are 40,000 units in San Francisco, California that are permitted, that are zoned, that are not being built because they can't afford to build them to a price that people can afford to buy them.
quote_candidates.speaker: Tom Steyer

quote_candidates.text: We should not believe that we have to build homes by making it so it's impossible for the carpenter who builds a home to never be able to afford to buy it.
quote_candidates.speaker: Javier Becerra

quote_candidates.text: The study is very, very clear that the speed is the driver.
quote_candidates.speaker: Katie Porter

quote_candidates.text: I am not scared of anybody because I've got three teenagers that I do not want living on my couch.
quote_candidates.speaker: Katie Porter

quote_candidates.text: We cut the fees by over two-thirds and 2,000 homes got under construction last year.
quote_candidates.speaker: Matt Mahan

guest_candidates.name: Tom Steyer
guest_candidates.title: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California

guest_candidates.name: Javier Becerra
guest_candidates.title: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California; former California Attorney General

guest_candidates.name: Katie Porter
guest_candidates.title: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California; U.S. Representative

guest_candidates.name: Matt Mahan
guest_candidates.title: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California; Mayor of San Jose

guest_candidates.name: Antonio Villaraigosa
guest_candidates.title: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California; former Mayor of Los Angeles
